beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check weather and work messages
beats.reasoning: Standard Tuesday morning routine at home. Yesterday's energy was solid (50/100) and today's grey drizzle calls for a grounded start before heading to Ballard.

beats.activity: Drive to work in Ballard via surface streets (avoiding SR-520 closure)
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; Nia avoids it by leaving early and taking local streets. Rain is steady but manageable. Budget 40 min for wet conditions and extra caution.

beats.activity: Warehouse logistics work: receiving, shipment organization, inventory coordination
beats.reasoning: Full morning and early afternoon shift. Steady workflow expected; Nia is pragmatic and focused, so rain outside doesn't disrupt her rhythm at the warehouse.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab soup and sandwich from a local café, eat indoors
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady until 2pm; Nia stays inside for lunch instead of the outdoor bench she used yesterday. Pragmatic choice given weather.

beats.activity: Afternoon warehouse shift: final shipments, closing inventory checks
beats.reasoning: Finish out the workday. By 2pm rain clears, but Nia stays focused on her logistics coordination tasks through the end of shift.

beats.activity: Commute home from Ballard via local streets
beats.reasoning: Post-work drive home. Rain has cleared; normal 35 min commute from Ballard to Fremont.

beats.activity: Change clothes, grab water, head to nearby trail for a 4K run
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's trail run cleared her head; today's rain has passed by 5:30pm and energy is still decent. Short run to shake off the day before settling in.
Post-run cooldown and freshen up. Nia is back at home base, ready to wind down for the evening.

beats.activity: Quick dinner: pasta with greens, herbal tea
beats.reasoning: Simple, nourishing meal at home. Nia prefers low-key evenings, especially after a focused work day and evening run.

beats.activity: Read mystery novel from Elliott Bay, sketch ideas for a painting project
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening: reading and creative work (painting sketches). Nia values quiet time alone with her interests; this balances yesterday's social energy and today's work focus.
Wind down to bed by 9:45pm; solid 8-hour sleep spans overnight into Wednesday morning. Ends at 6am as required.

diary: Woke up to grey skies and my phone buzzing with closure alerts. SR-520 was down, so I took the long way through Ballard—surface streets all the way. The rain was steady, kind of meditative. Work was solid; got through receiving and inventory without any major hiccups, though everyone was a bit tense from the traffic chaos. Grabbed soup at Ballard Market Café around lunch and just sat there watching the rain streak the windows. Afternoon flew by. Got home, changed into running gear almost immediately—I needed to move, to shake off the warehouse energy. The Fremont Greenway was quiet and wet, perfect for clearing my head. Four kilometers felt exactly right. After a shower and some pasta, I spent the evening with my mystery novel and sketching. Started mapping out colors for a new painting I've been thinking about. There's something about rainy days that makes me want to create instead of scroll. Feeling settled now, ready for sleep.
thought_process: The bridge closure threw me off initially, but I made a quick decision to avoid the main arterial and trust the surface streets—logistics training kicks in everywhere, I guess. Work felt purposeful; I stayed focused on the inventory checks because accuracy matters and I take that seriously. The café stop was deliberate; I needed to sit with quiet for a bit instead of eating at my desk. By afternoon, though, I felt restless. The grey weather wasn't depressing exactly, but it built up this energy in me. Running was the right call—physical exertion clears the mental clutter better than anything. Dinner and the novel were intentional decompress time, but then I found myself sketching instead of just zoning out with a screen. The painting project has been nagging at me, and the rainy mood seemed to unlock something creatively. I'm ending the day feeling grounded, which is rare after warehouse logistics work.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
